patterns: what are the symptoms of diabetes
responses: Common symptoms of diabetes include increased thirst, frequent urination, extreme hunger, unexplained weight loss, fatigue, irritability, blurred vision, and slow-healing sores. If you suspect diabetes, please consult a doctor for a blood test.
A fever is a temporary increase in your body temperature. If your fever is 103°F (39.4°C) or higher, or lasts more than three days, you should consult a doctor. Stay hydrated and rest.
Headaches can be caused by dehydration, stress, lack of sleep, or poor diet. Try drinking water and resting in a quiet, dark room. If severe, sudden, or persistent, see a doctor immediately.

Normal blood pressure is typically around 120/80 mmHg. High blood pressure (hypertension) often has no symptoms, so regular checkups are important. It can increase the risk of heart disease and stroke.
Common COVID-19 symptoms include fever, dry cough, fatigue, loss of taste or smell, and shortness of breath. If you have severe symptoms like difficulty breathing, seek immediate medical attention.

Asthma symptoms include shortness of breath, chest tightness or pain, wheezing when exhaling, and trouble sleeping caused by shortness of breath. Use your inhaler as prescribed or seek emergency care if an attack is severe.

Stomach aches can be caused by indigestion, gas, food poisoning, or stress. Drink plenty of clear liquids. If the pain is severe, accompanied by persistent vomiting or fever, consult a healthcare provider.

The common cold or flu often presents with a runny or stuffy nose, sore throat, cough, congestion, slight body aches, or a mild headache. Rest, drink plenty of fluids, and try over-the-counter remedies. Consult a doctor if symptoms worsen.
